falsify: If source protocol cannot be identified or random/official split differences are negligible across several targets.
name: benchmark protocol extraction and validation
notFramework: It is target-bound: every output must run on a named external dataset.
test: Extract source protocol, implement official split, compare against random split, publish divergence.
why: The biggest repeated limitation in Batch 9 and Batch 11 was controlled split evaluation without official protocol reproduction.

falsify: If it only restates sklearn split parameters and finds no measurable divergence.
name: split-risk detector
notFramework: It is a small analysis instrument attached to executed benchmark targets.
test: Run official, random, stratified, and group-like splits where source metadata allows.
why: Vehicle fallback, Dry Bean split limits, and Optical contributor splits show split choice can dominate interpretation.

falsify: If ordinary pandas checks catch every case with no added packaging or risk ranking.
name: leakage-risk auditor
notFramework: It is only accepted if tested on positive and negative leakage controls.
test: Inject known leakage controls and detect them before applying to public targets.
why: Current tools cannot prove absence of leakage; a scoped auditor could look for target-coded columns, duplicate crossing, and group leakage.

falsify: If dynamic tests still make counts unreliable without target-specific manual work.
name: repo test execution summarizer v2
notFramework: It must be built only inside a repo/test reproduction batch.
test: Compare AST inventory, pytest collection, and executed cases across several public repos.
why: pytest_repro_summary is support-only until static inventory is reconciled with runtime collection.

falsify: If controls do not change conclusions beyond existing metric stress reports.
name: negative-control generator
notFramework: It is evaluated only through public target runs and baseline-dominance checks.
test: Generate shuffled-label, permuted-feature, duplicate-crossing, and majority-class controls for executed targets.
why: metric_stress_validator is useful because it forces controls; broader negative controls could prevent overclaiming.

falsify: If manual source cards remain faster and more reliable.
name: dataset-card-to-execution-plan extractor
notFramework: It produces no standalone platform; it emits target-specific reproduction plans.
test: Extract execution plans from UCI pages and verify against actual loaded files.
why: Source metadata often names files, splits, and limits that controlled split scripts ignore.

falsify: If selected baselines are weaker than obvious hand-picked baselines.
name: baseline-dominance challenge generator
notFramework: It is accepted only when it runs against concrete external benchmarks.
test: Automatically choose dummy, linear, tree, and source-protocol baselines for a public target.
why: Batch 5 and later batches show simple baselines are the best anti-hype guard.
